channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The State of AI Adoption in Asia Pacific: Leaders and Followers
item.description: AI adoption in Asia Pacific, leaders, followers, strategies, challenges
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Google unveils Gemini 2.0 AI model for agentic era
item.description: Emphasizing a new AI model for the agentic era, Google has introduced Gemini 2.0, which the company calls its most capable model yet. Announced December 11, the Gemini 2.0 Flash experimental model will be available to all Gemini users.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Internet rankings are breaking our brains
item.description: Any time I’m unsure of which jacket to buy, or which movie to watch, or which country to visit, I do this one simple trick: take a look at the rankings. One need not look very far to come across a ranked list of products and services on any given day.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: New approach to Rocky Mountain grizzlies sought ahead of the second Trump presidency
item.description: Environmental groups are asking the U.S. government to do more to protect grizzly bears in Wyoming, Montana and Idaho.
item.dc:creator: Mead Gruver, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: You can now buy a new car on Amazon – as long as it's a Hyundai
item.description: Amazon's online car-shopping channel has officially launched, with users now able to purchase Hyundai products directly from the website.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Pentagon announces new AI office as it looks to deploy autonomous weapons
item.description: The Defense Department on Wednesday announced a new office focused on accelerating and adopting artificial intelligence (AI) technology for the military as it aims to deploy autonomous weapons in the near future.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Google claims quantum leap with new Willow chip
item.description: The breakthrough potential of quantum computers remains a ways off due to two crucial issues: error correction and computing power. In addition, the quality of qubits — aka quantum bits, the basic units of information for quantum computing — is not yet sufficient for longer calculations.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Police departments are embracing AI-powered video surveillance. This company is leading the effort
item.description: A red Hyundai SUV makes its way through downtown San Francisco on a bright August afternoon. The car looks innocuous, but a day earlier, police had linked it to the break-in of another car and added it to a so-called hot list of wanted vehicles.
item.dc:creator: Ainsley Harris, Fast Company

item.title: Rose Wang says Bluesky is so much more than an X clone
item.description: A huge shift in social media is underway: People are leaving X while millions have flocked to Bluesky, a new upstart powered by a tiny 20-person team. Bluesky’s COO, Rose Wang, addresses this inflection point as dissatisfaction with major platforms continues to grow.
item.dc:creator: Robert Safian, Fast Company

item.title: Supreme Court allows investors' class action to proceed against microchip company Nvidia
item.description: The Supreme Court is allowing a class-action lawsuit that accuses Nvidia of misleading investors about its past dependence on selling computer chips for the mining of volatile cryptocurrency to proceed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: California defense contractors optimistic Trump administration could create jobs
item.description: The Reagan National Defense Survey done just after the November election found that 79% of those surveyed want the U.S. to spend more on national defense.
item.dc:creator: Erika I. Ritchie, The Mercury News

item.title: TikTok files challenge against Canadian government order to dissolve its business in the country
item.description: TikTok has challenged a Canadian government order to shut down the Chinese video-sharing app’s business operations in the country that was imposed over national security concerns.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 8 brilliant browser tab tricks for Windows power users
item.description: Web browsers are essential for everything from business productivity to social media and online shopping. Whether you use Google Chrome, Microsoft Edge, Mozilla Firefox, Brave, or something even more exotic, there’s a good chance your web browser is your most used Windows program.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Why Meta shareholders are seeking sanctions for former COO Sheryl Sandberg
item.description: Attorneys for Meta shareholders asked a Delaware judge Monday to sanction the company’s former Chief Operating Officer Sheryl Sandberg and fellow Facebook board member and current White House chief of staff Jeff Zients for deleting emails related to the Cambridge Analytica privacy scandal, despite b
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Fortnite players 'tricked' into unwanted purchases are starting to get refunds. Here's how to apply
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. consumers who were “tricked” into purchases they didn't want from Fortnite maker Epic Games are now starting to receive refund checks, the Federal Trade Commission said this week.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bay Area lawmaker introduces bill requiring mental health warnings on social media sites
item.description: “The promise of social media, although real, has turned into a situation where they’re turning our children’s attention into a commodity,” Assemblymember Rebecca Bauer-Kahan, who authored the California bill, said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Kylie Kelce unseats Joe Rogan as top podcast with debut episode
item.description: Kylie Kelce, wife of NFL player-turned-media personality Jason Kelce, beat out Joe Rogan for the top podcast spot on both Apple and Spotify this week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: Setapp survey reveals what Mac developers are thinking about right now
item.description: Mac developers have a lot to think about as we close in on 2025, the eighth Mac developer survey from Setapp suggests.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: OpenAI expands multimodal capabilities with updated text-to-video model
item.description: OpenAI has released a new version of its text-to-video AI model, Sora, for ChatGPT Plus and Pro users, marking another step in expansion into multimodal AI technologies.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Nvidia's stock dips after China opens probe of the AI chip company for violating anti-monopoly laws
item.description: Shares of Nvidia fell Monday after China said it is investigating the high-flying U.S. microchip company over suspected violations of Chinese anti-monopoly laws.
item.dc:creator: MATT OTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NaaS vendor Graphiant launches data assurance service to boost network visibility and compliance
item.description: Graphiant is expanding its network-as-a-service (NaaS) platform today with new data assurance capabilities in a bid to help organizations meet the challenges of data sovereignty and regulatory compliance.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: IAEA chief: Iran is poised to 'quite dramatically' increase stockpile of near weapons-grade uranium
item.description: MANAMA, Bahrain (AP) — Iran is poised to “quite dramatically” increase its stockpile of near weapons-grade uranium as it has started cascades of advanced centrifuges, the head of the International Atomic Energy Agency warned Friday.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal appeals court upholds law requiring sale or ban of TikTok in the US
item.description: A federal appeals court panel on Friday unanimously upheld a law that could lead to a ban on TikTok as soon as next month, handing a resounding defeat to the popular social media platform as it fights for its survival in the U.S. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Better together? Why AWS is unifying data analytics and AI services in SageMaker
item.description: Data warehousing, business intelligence, data analytics, and AI services are all coming together under one roof at Amazon Web Services.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: China's ban on key high-tech materials could have broad impact on industries, economy
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China has banned exports of key materials used to make a wide range of products, including smartphones, electric vehicles, radar systems and CT scanners, swiping back at Washington after it expanded export controls to include dozens of Chinese companies that make equipment used to pro
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KRON4
